content:
Key Findings

  • Fylde Borough Council spent a total of £157.4M across 37,514 transactions between 2015/16 and 2025/26
  • Spending patterns show significant variations throughout the financial year
  • The council's spending habits reveal distinct timing patterns that could impact budget management

Year-End Spending Spikes

Fylde Borough Council's spending data reveals notable fluctuations throughout the financial year. One key pattern is the increase in expenditure towards the end of each financial year. This trend is common across many councils as they aim to utilise their allocated budgets before the year-end. The total spend of £157.4M across 37,514 transactions highlights the scale of financial activity managed by the council.

The end-of-year spending spike is often driven by the need to avoid budget rollover or to complete projects within the current financial framework. This practice ensures that funds are used efficiently and that the council meets its annual financial targets. Understanding these patterns can help both the council and the public better anticipate and plan for financial commitments.

Day-of-Week Distributions

The distribution of spending across different days of the week also shows interesting patterns. Typically, councils process payments on specific days to manage cash flow and administrative efficiency. For Fylde Borough Council, the data suggests that certain days may see higher transaction volumes, which could be due to internal processing schedules or external factors such as supplier payment terms.

Analysing these day-of-week patterns can provide insights into the council's operational efficiency. For instance, if most transactions occur on particular days, it might indicate bottlenecks or optimised processing times. This information can be useful for both the council's financial planning and for suppliers who need to align their invoicing and payment schedules with the council's practices.

Seasonal Variations in Spending

Seasonal variations in spending are another critical aspect of Fylde Borough Council's financial management. Certain services and projects may require increased funding during specific times of the year. For example, winter maintenance, holiday events, or seasonal staffing adjustments can lead to fluctuations in expenditure. The total spend of £157.4M across 37,514 transactions underscores the importance of understanding these seasonal trends.

By identifying and planning for these seasonal variations, the council can ensure that resources are allocated effectively and that services are maintained without financial strain. This proactive approach can help in avoiding last-minute budget adjustments and ensure that the council's financial health remains stable throughout the year.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Fylde Borough Council about the specific reasons behind year-end spending spikes
  • Attend council committee meetings, which are open to the public, to discuss budget management and spending patterns
  • Check the council's published contracts register to understand the timing and nature of major expenditures
  • Write to your local councillor about concerns regarding seasonal variations in spending and their impact on local services
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and gain deeper insights into Fylde Borough Council's financial activities
This article is based on Fylde Borough Council's published spending data covering 2015/16 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
